rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when bounds are concrete or visible
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings with valid output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
generic_rules: Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Align helper declaration types with the expression types for valid comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq for equation declarations and guarantee for property guarantees
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings with valid output features or auxiliary variables
Use bool for comparison expressions, int for integer literals and arithmetic, and real for real literals and arithmetic
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
